steps.title: Identify the pattern
steps.content: Both examples follow the same pathway: an Indigenous language (Nahuatl for “coyote,” Quechua for “llama”) → Spanish → English. The passage is consistently showing Spanish as an intermediary.

steps.title: Determine Spanish's function
steps.content: This pathway—Indigenous language → Spanish → English—is precisely what “serving as a medium” means. The other choices either misrepresent Spanish's connection to Indigenous languages (A claims a reciprocal exchange; D treats Spanish as an equivalent contributor rather than an intermediary) or overgeneralize from two examples (B). That matches choice C — C is correct.
